title: Only cache repeated structures
description: Compression currently caches every cacheable structure, including values that occur only once. Single-use entries increase output size and make expand slower. Add a repeated-only compression pass so cache entries are emitted only for structures seen more than once, while preserving cache-0 as the root.
acceptance_criteria: de-dupe/de-dupe-eq round-trip existing cases; cache output for mixed unique/repeated data is smaller; tests prove unique child structures are not extracted while repeated structures still preserve sharing.
close_reason: Implemented two-pass repeated-only extraction so unique cacheable structures stay inline while repeated structures preserve sharing.

title: Make de-dupe-eq collision-safe
description: de-dupe-eq uses cljs hash values as unique js/Map keys. Hash collisions can cause unequal structures to be represented by the same cache token and corrupt expanded data. Store collision buckets and compare with = within each bucket.
acceptance_criteria: de-dupe-eq only reuses a token when values are =; a forced hash-collision test with unequal values round-trips correctly.
close_reason: Changed equality-mode cache lookup to use hash buckets plus = comparison and added forced-collision coverage.

title: Use serializable cache tokens
description: de-dupe stores cache-token identity in symbol metadata. The README advertises Transit/EDN serialization, but normal serialization does not preserve symbol metadata, so expand can fail after a real serialized round-trip. Replace metadata-dependent token detection with a serializable token representation and cover serialized round-trips in tests.
acceptance_criteria: Cache tokens survive pr-str/read-string style round-trips; expand(de-dupe(x)) still preserves equality and sharing; tests cover serialized cache data.
close_reason: Replaced metadata-dependent cache token format with serializable namespaced symbols and added printed serialization round-trip coverage.

title: Short-circuit cache-token detection
description: contains-compressed-elements? uses flatten, which fully realizes nested data and cannot stop at the first token. Replace it with a short-circuiting tree predicate.
acceptance_criteria: Tests still pass; contains-compressed-elements? detects nested cache tokens and does not treat normal data as cache tokens.
close_reason: Replaced flatten-based token search with a short-circuiting recursive predicate.

title: Memoize recursive cache expansion
description: decompress-cache repeatedly partitions and walks unresolved cache entries. Replace or supplement it with recursive memoized expansion so each cache entry is expanded once.
acceptance_criteria: expand preserves equality and sharing; tests cover nested cache dependencies; benchmark expand path improves or at least avoids repeated full-cache passes.
close_reason: Replaced repeated decompression passes with memoized recursive cache expansion; benchmark expand path now runs in tens of milliseconds on the fixture.

title: Modernize test and build toolchain
description: The project uses old Leiningen/cljsbuild/cemerick.cljs.test/SlimerJS tooling. Replace or supplement it with a modern non-browser test path that runs in CI-friendly environments.
acceptance_criteria: A documented command runs unit tests non-interactively on the local machine; obsolete SlimerJS test dependency is no longer required for the primary test path.
close_reason: Added deps.edn/package scripts for Node-based cljs.test execution and removed SlimerJS/cljsbuild as the primary test path.

title: Add compression size benchmark checks
description: Optimization work needs visible feedback. Add benchmark output for raw size, compressed size, eq compressed size, and cache entry counts so size/performance tradeoffs are easy to inspect.
acceptance_criteria: npm run bench reports cache entry counts and size ratios for de-dupe and de-dupe-eq.
close_reason: Benchmark now reports cache entry counts, compressed string lengths, and compressed/raw ratios for de-dupe and de-dupe-eq.

title: Fix stale project metadata and docs
description: project.clj still points at structural-caching URLs and non-existent main namespace, README references Travis and has stale API/tooling guidance. Update metadata and documentation to match the current repo and build/test commands.
acceptance_criteria: Project metadata references day8/de-dupe and existing namespaces; README documents current APIs, limitations, and test command.
close_reason: Updated project metadata, README, CI config, and documented modern test/benchmark commands.

title: Consider size threshold for extraction
description: Even repeated tiny values may be larger when extracted into a cache entry plus refs. Investigate an optional threshold or size heuristic for extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Document whether to add a threshold now; if implemented, tests cover repeated tiny structures and repeated large structures.
close_reason: Evaluated during optimization pass. Repeated-only extraction already removes the biggest size overhead. Deferring size thresholds because tiny repeated values need corpus-driven heuristics to avoid surprising behavior changes.

title: Evaluate compact public cache format
description: Namespaced symbol tokens are readable and serialization-safe but verbose. Evaluate a compact public representation such as {:de-dupe/root 0 :de-dupe/cache {0 ...}} or reserved ref forms, including collision risks with user data.
acceptance_criteria: Document a recommended compact format or close with rationale for keeping namespaced symbols.
close_reason: Evaluated during optimization pass. Kept namespaced-symbol token format for now because it is serialization-safe, readable, and avoids a breaking public cache-shape change. Revisit a compact {:root :cache} format only with an explicit compatibility plan.

title: Repair benchmark build path
description: The benchmark namespace and browser page still use stale wiring: bench code calls expand unqualified and index_bench requires the test namespace. Modernize or repair the benchmark command so it can run after the toolchain update.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmark code compiles under the modern toolchain and documented benchmark command points at the bench namespace.
close_reason: Fixed benchmark namespace/wiring and added npm run bench through the modern CLJS Node path.

title: Remove library load side effects
description: The library namespace calls enable-console-print! at load time. Libraries should not mutate global console/print behavior just by being required.
acceptance_criteria: de-dupe.core can be required without calling enable-console-print!; tests still run and handle their own output setup.
close_reason: Removed enable-console-print! from the library namespace; output setup remains in test/bench namespaces.
